STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE
ORDER GRANTING EX PARTE APPLICATION AND STIPULATION IN SUPPORT OF ORDER CONTINUING JURY TRIAL DATE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. STANDING IS NOT NECESSARILY LIMITED TO THOSE PERSONS WHO HAVE THE STATUTORY RIGHT TO CONTROL DISPOSITION OF THE REMAINS. HERE DEFENDANT WAS ITSELF THE PARTY THAT IS ACCUSED OF DISTURBING THE REMAINS. (SEE CHRISTENSEN V. SUP.CT. (1991) 54 CAL.3D 868, 875.) FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER UCSF MAINTAINED THE APPLICABLE STANDARD OF CARE IN PERFORMING THE AUTOPSY AS PROFFERED BY THE DECLARATIONS OF DEFENDANT'S EXPERT DR. ROSENTHAL AND PLAINTIFFS' EXPERT DR. BRONSTON. (FLOWERS V. TORRANCE MEM'L HOSP. MED. CTR. (1994) 8 CAL.4TH 992, 1001; KELLEY V. TRUNK (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 519, 524.) FURTHER, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER THERE WAS FRAUD, OVERREACHING OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT TO EXCUSE ESPERANZA COTA ROMERO'S EXECUTION OF THE AUTOPSY PERMIT WITHOUT FIRST READING THE INSTRUMENT BEFORE SIGNING IT. (STEWART V. PRESTON PIPELINE INC. (2005) 134 CAL.APP.4TH 1565, 1588.) FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT AS A PUBLIC ENTITY, IT IS IMMUNE FROM LIABILITY BASED ON A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD WITH RESPECT TO CONDUCTING AN AUTOPSY GIVEN CASE LAW LIMITING THE IMMUNITY TO INSTANCES OF FINANCIAL OR COMMERCIAL FRAUD. (GOV. CODE 818.8; 5 WITKIN, CAL. SUMMARY (10TH ED. 2005) TORTS, 242, P. 398.) PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO STRIKE DR. ROSENTHAL'S DECLARATION IS DENIED. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Submit Tardy Expert Information And Motion For Sanctions Or Alternatively Motion For Relief From Error And Motion For Leave To Complete Expert Deposition And Motion For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Advanced From Nov-26-13 To Nov-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANTONIO RODRIGUEZ, ESQ., 559.445.0123, FOR PLAINTIFF ESPERANZA ROMERO.
JOHN ETCHEVERS, ESQ., 415.233.9800 X223, FOR DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Standing is not necessarily limited to those persons who have the statutory right to control disposition of the remains. Here Defendant was itself the party that is accused of disturbing the remains. (See Christensen v. Sup.Ct. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 868, 875.) For the first cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether UCSF maintained the applicable standard of care in performing the autopsy as proffered by the declarations of Defendant's expert Dr. Rosenthal and Plaintiffs' expert Dr. Bronston. (Flowers v. Torrance Mem'l Hosp. Med. Ctr. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001; Kelley v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 524.) Further, there are triable issues of fact whether there was fraud, overreaching or excusable neglect to excuse Esperanza Cota Romero's execution of the Autopsy Permit without first reading the instrument before signing it. (Stewart v. Preston Pipeline Inc. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1565, 1588.) For the second cause of action, Defendant has not met its initial burden to demonstrate that as a public entity, it is immune from liability based on a cause of action for fraud with respect to conducting an autopsy given case law limiting the immunity to instances of financial or commercial fraud. (Gov. Code 818.8; 5 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Torts, 242, p. 398.) Plaintiffs' motion to strike Dr. Rosenthal's declaration is denied. The objections are overruled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
